title: The Twentieth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## October 27, 2019 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon falls on the Twentieth Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time, following the traditional ecclesiastical calendar. Under the Revised Common Lectionary (RCL) for Year C, the readings would have been Habakkuk 1:1-4, 2:1-4 (or Joel 2:23-32); Psalm 65; 2 Timothy 4:6-8, 16-18; and Luke 18:9-14, the parable of the Pharisee and the tax collector. The church was in the extended post-Pentecost season, roughly six weeks before Advent begins on December 1st.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in a period of relative calm following the 78th General Convention in July 2018 in Austin, where the church had approved the use of more inclusive language for God in proposed prayer book revisions. In Colorado, the Diocese of Colorado (which includes Saint John's Cathedral in Denver) was led by Bishop Rob Saylor, who had been serving since 2017 and was focused on reconciliation and justice work. No major diocesan controversies or leadership transitions were occurring in late October 2019.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical early autumn weather with temperatures in the 50s-60s Fahrenheit. The city's major news focus in late October centered on holiday preparations and the approaching ski season. The University of Colorado football team was in mid-season play (a period of significant local sports interest), and Denver's major civic attention was beginning to turn toward the 2020 election cycle, with Colorado positioned as a key swing state.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The impeachment inquiry into President Trump dominated national news, with the House Intelligence Committee holding televised hearings on Ukraine that week (October 23 and later dates). The United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) was advancing toward ratification after replacing NAFTA. The stock market was volatile amid ongoing trade war concerns with China. The 2020 Democratic primary was heating up with Iowa caucuses less than three months away.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Syrian government, with Russian support, was intensifying military operations in northwest Syria following a Turkish incursion into Kurdish-held territory that had begun in early October. The Hong Kong pro-democracy protests continued in their fifth month with escalating violence between demonstrators and police. The United Kingdom remained in political turmoil over Brexit, with Prime Minister Boris Johnson negotiating a new withdrawal agreement that would be put to Parliament within days (October 28, 2019).

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in a state of deep political polarization and anxiety, with the impeachment process consuming media and public discourse. The broader cultural moment reflected heightened skepticism toward institutions and significant generational divides. For Denver specifically, the Rockies baseball season had just ended (missing the playoffs), and attention was turning to the Broncos' middling NFL season and the upcoming winter holiday season. There was an undercurrent of election-year tension mixed with ordinary autumn routine.
